title: BACKND Base game information data flows
summary: Covers shared game-data table operations in BACKND Base, including table discovery, insert, query patterns, owner-focused reads, updates, calculations, deletes, and transaction-based workflows.
capabilities: route questions about shared gameplay data storage to the correct document family
explain the difference between simple CRUD calls and transaction-based workflows
identify when query, inDate, or owner-focused access patterns are likely needed
highlight that table design and schema choices affect API behavior and validation
prerequisites: SDK initialization and login are already complete when user-scoped behavior matters
The target table exists in the BACKND console
The caller knows whether the table is intended for shared game data or per-user data
common_pitfalls: Using game-information routes when the data model should really be stored in player-data
Treating transaction examples as interchangeable with single-call CRUD flows without checking the current API page
Assuming schema and null-handling behavior without verifying the official docs for the target table design
verification_strategy: Use https://docs.backnd.com plus the narrower source route before generating code for CRUD or transaction flows.
Treat this topic as a route selector and workflow summary, not as a frozen API contract.
public_notes: Agents should choose a narrower child route before generating code, because this route family spans CRUD, transactions, and query-building patterns.
Exact method names and request shapes should be rechecked from the official docs when producing implementation-ready code.
